title: Pigmented lesion review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

referral.text: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.
referral.rationale: The exact skin-cancer recognition section supports documenting a changing pigmented lesion, interval change, symptoms, clinician skin inspection, distribution or site, inflammation, oozing and sensation change without asserting malignancy or generating referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

referral.text: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.
referral.rationale: The exact melanoma-assessment section supports documenting clinical photographs reviewed when available and consented, and a clinician-completed lesion assessment; it does not create dermoscopic findings or diagnosis.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

follow_up.text: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.
follow_up.rationale: The exact safety-netting section supports recording clinician-discussed safety net and arranged follow-up only.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

sources_used.title: Melanoma: assessment and management — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation

source_limitations: Exact site, dimensions, morphology, dermoscopy, skin type, sun exposure, family and personal cancer history, prior images, diagnosis, biopsy or excision decision, pathology, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
